trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Canada exports canola, wheat, lumber, and mining equipment to Malesia. Malesia exports electronics, palm oil, machinery, and chemicals to Canada. Malesia is an important CPTPP partner.

diplomatic.summary: Constructive relations as Commonwealth partners and CPTPP members. Canada and Malesia cooperate in APEC. Both have an interest in a stable Indo-Pacific. Canada is cautious about Malesian human rights record.

military.summary: Modest military ties through Commonwealth frameworks. Canada has provided some training to Malesian forces. Limited defense cooperation.

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Canadian mining companies have some Malesian market presence. Petronas has explored Canadian LNG opportunities. Growing interest in critical minerals.

energy.summary: Malesia imports some Canadian LNG. Canada sees Malesia as a potential buyer of Canadian energy exports. Petronas is active in Canadian LNG discussions.

scientific.summary: Limited but growing scientific cooperation. Some Malesian students study in Canada. Joint research in tropical ecology and IT.
